prose:
Method–provenance summary (reproducible):
Property: gas-phase HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans) gap of aniline, in eV.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitting) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (aniline.xyz); single-point only, no further optimisation (DFT//input-xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: PySCF (version as in chemkit backend).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 9 cycles. No engine warnings were issued. Integrity check reports status “ok”, trustworthy = true.

Headline result
• HOMO = –5.553772 eV
• LUMO = –0.2489544 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV  (value field above)
Additional orbitals, Koopmans IP/EA, and the full JSON are available in /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmpwa7w9rao/live.json. The live engine log was streamed to frontier-orbitals_<timestamp>.out in the working directory; retain it for step-by-step SCF details.

The figure reported is purely computational; no experimental comparison has been attempted. Anyone can reproduce it with the CLI shown in the result JSON:
/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard aniline.xyz --out <out.json>
